what_it_does: A build-lane report (machine-readable JSON) recording the construction and verification of the 'verify_lens' role: which files were written, the new role id, an extensive 'how_verified' narrative, build status, needs_tim flag, and a long 'notes' field carrying design decisions and handoff instructions for the next build beat.
observations: how_verified describes RoleRegistry().discover(['roles']) showing the role live (can_fire=True, is_jury=False, op=generate, in 'verification' cast only)
Verified by-use via run_items over 2 lens-units (correctness + adversarial-disprove) against a mock buggy add() on a resident chat-4b model (~8GB GPU resident)
Reports two lens verdicts: correctness verdict=fail ('returns a-b not the sum'), adversarial-disprove verdict=fail with breaking_case='add(5,3) returns 2, not 8'

Claims tests/cognition_governance_acceptance.py = 42/42 PASS and tests/roles_acceptance.py passes all structural checks then fails at exactly line 174 'drift: [verify_lens]' (un-reflected-role drift assertion), described as EXPECTED
notes carries the unit contract for the next beat: each unit is {lens, change, bar}; insists key is 'change' DELIBERATELY (not COMPOSITIONS example's 'diff')
Documents that THE TALLY (verdict-tally reduce-rule) is deferred to the next beat in mcp_face's _REDUCE_RULES, not built here to avoid collision
verdict is Literal['pass','fail','uncertain']; breaking_case defaults to ''; mode_scope={'verification'}; input_addresses stays default ('utterance',)
Did NOT commit and did NOT edit roles/AGENTS.md (per brief); by-use harness left at /tmp/verify_lens_byuse.py
standouts: The verify-by-use trace: a deliberately buggy add() driven through two distinct lenses, each returning a structured {lens,verdict,evidence,breaking_case}
Explicit warning that the next-beat cascade must pass key 'change' not the doc's 'diff', or it mismatches the prompt
An expected-red test (roles_acceptance line 174) is documented as a non-defect drift that the next beat's reflection turns green
conventions: Follows a lane-report JSON shape (lane, files_written, new_role_ids, how_verified, status, needs_tim, notes)
Carries cross-beat handoff state inside a free-text notes field rather than structured fields
Uses the repo's verification-by-use discipline (fire the role, observe verdicts) and exact-acceptance-test counts as evidence
notes: A build-lane provenance record for the verify_lens role, heavy on verification narrative and next-beat handoff instructions packed into one notes string.
questions: Where does the next-beat tally reduce-rule actually land, and has roles/AGENTS.md since been reflected to clear the line-174 drift?
Is the /tmp/verify_lens_byuse.py harness preserved anywhere durable, or was it ephemeral?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it is a verification/handoff report for a build lane and reads exactly as that.
apparent_themes: cross-lens fan-out as a non-jury verification mechanism
file-disjoint lane discipline to avoid collisions between concurrent builds
closed-vocabulary verdicts feeding a downstream deterministic tally
intention_signals: Recording, with strong evidentiary discipline, that a new verification role was built and proven by firing it against a known-bad change, while explicitly fencing off the parts (tally, AGENTS reflection, commit) belonging to other lanes/beats.
novelty: The 'verify_lens' design as a cross-LENS fan (N distinct lenses, one draw each via run_items) deliberately NOT implemented as a draws-jury, with a mode_scope cast beyond the listening cast.
contribution: Contributes a verified provenance record and design rationale for a per-lens verification role, including the precise unit contract {lens,change,bar} and the deferred-tally seam that a fused system would keep as the verification-organ's wiring memory.
summary_for_embedding: A build-lane JSON report documenting that the verify_lens role was built and verified by-use through two distinct lenses against a known-buggy function, with deferred-tally and next-beat handoff notes.
intention_for_embedding: To record evidence-backed completion of the verify_lens verification role and hand the tally/reflection/commit work to the next build beat.
